name: Bow Tie Pasta with Blackened Scallops
recipeInstructions:
Note: Seafood Stock can be made from fish-flavored bouillon cubes,
available in most supermarkets.
Bring a large pot of water to a boil over high heat.
In the meantime, make the pepper sauce. Put the roasted peppers,
shallots, garlic and vinegar in a blender and puree until smooth. Add
1/2 cup of the seafood stock and the basil and mix until blended.
Transfer the mixture to a small saucepan, cover, and cook for 5 minutes over low heat.
For the cilantro pesto, put the walnuts, garlic and cilantro in the
bowl of a food processor. Turn the machine on and add 1 tablespoon
of the seafood stock through the feed tube. Drizzle in the lemon
juice to form a paste. Transfer the contents of the food processor to
a small mixing bowl and whisk in another 3 tablespoons of the stock.
When the water comes to a boil, add the pasta to the pot. Cook for 8
to 10 minutes, until al dente. Drain.
Dust the scallops with the blackening seasoning, paprika and thyme.
Heat a heavy frying pan over medium heat. Spray the pan with vegetable oil.
Add the prepared scallops and sear them for 2 minutes on each side.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and cook for an additional 5 minutes until the scallops are firm.
Return the drained pasta to the pot. Over low heat stir in the cilantro pesto.
Divide the pasta among 4 bowls. Place 4 scallops on top of each and pour the pepper sauce over the scallops.
